description.txt: A workspace Owner using Soleur as their AI organization. Workspaces may have MULTIPLE Owners (ADR-038, ADR-073 team workspaces); Owner-shared surfaces (e.g. the operator email-triage inbox, ADR-066) are readable by every Owner, not just one founder.
Vendors, regulators, and counsel emailing the operator address. Their statutory/operational mail is the email-triage inbox's inbound source.

A beta tester or prospect the operator is in sales/product conversations with. The origin of the third-party contact + conversation PII captured in the owner-private beta-CRM (beta_contacts / interview_notes). An involuntary data subject (Art. 14); no direct access to the store.

An untrusted contributor opening a pull request. Their PR-head code is executed only by the fix-constraints Stage A producer (pull_request, contents:read, no write token) — never by the privileged Stage B consumer, which opens a data-only draft follow-up PR from the default branch (ADR-074).
Claude LLM for agent reasoning and tool use
Source control, CI/CD, issue tracking, and releases
DNS, CDN, R2 storage, and zero-trust tunnel
Centralized secrets management with runtime injection
Community notifications and release announcements
Payment processing and subscription billing
Privacy-focused website analytics
Inbound email ingress (operator-inbox webhook, svix-verified) + transactional outbound (statutory triage notifications). ADR-066.

PRIVATE GHCR registry (ghcr.io/jikig-ai/soleur-web-platform + soleur-inngest-bootstrap) — the ORIGINAL host pull source, now the DUAL-PUSH + break-glass FALLBACK during the migration to self-hosted zot (ADR-096, #6122); retired from the pull path after the Phase-5 soak (App tokens can't pull GHCR, so it cannot deliver a zero-touch machine credential — ADR-088 superseded). Still bakes the host bootstrap scripts + hooks.json.tmpl, extracted via docker cp at first boot to keep cloud-init user_data under Hetzner's 32,768-byte cap (ADR-080, #5921).

OCI-native container registry (zot) on a dedicated Hetzner host (cx33, 4 vCPU / 8 GB, hel1, volume-backed) on the private network (10.0.1.30:5000), behind a deny-all-public firewall — the zero-touch machine-credential registry that replaces GHCR on the pull path (ADR-096, #6122). Sized cx33 in hel1 (#6288 — the plan's cx32 target does not exist as a Hetzner type; cx33 is the real 8 GB type, offered in hel1 not nbg1) with an ADR-062 --memory=7168m cap so zot's ~35 GB store boot scan cannot host-OOM restart-loop the box. The 60 GB store volume is a disposable GHCR mirror: the nbg1→hel1 migration recreates it fresh and it re-fills from GHCR on the next CI dual-push. Hosts pull the SAME @sha256 digests here (crane-copy from CI preserves them); cosign verifies the zot-stored .sig offline against the unchanged pinned root. Plain-HTTP on the private net (integrity via cosign digest-pinning, not TLS); dark-launch gated with an atomic GHCR fallback until the Phase-5 soak retires GHCR push.

External uptime/heartbeat monitoring (BetterStackHQ/better-uptime) AND a Logs warehouse (source 2457081, ClickHouse-SQL-queryable) that CI polls read-only. Apex + inngest/git-data heartbeats, and a per-host origin absence detector that probes each web host's CF-proxied hostname (web-N.app.soleur.ai/health) directly, so a dead/never-booted host pages with per-host attribution the round-robin monitors cannot give (#5933 Item 1). The Logs source receives SOLEUR_ZOT_DISK self-reports from the deny-all registry host and is polled from GitHub Actions via betterstack-query.sh for the zot restart-loop recurrence alarm (#6291) + follow-through soak probes — log-content recurrence alarms are in-repo GH-cron pollers, not native Better Stack alerts (ADR-096).

Keyless container-image signing/verification (cosign/Fulcio/Rekor). The release CI keyless-signs the pushed web app image digest (this IS a live sigstore call — github → sigstore below). The host then cosign-verifies the signature FULLY OFFLINE against a pinned trusted_root.json — it makes NO live sigstore call at verify time (ADR-087, #6005) — supply-chain authenticity for the shared host (#5933 Item 4).

The connected repo's own committed plugins/soleur/ dir, present in the user's workspace when they connect a Soleur-dogfooding repo. UNTRUSTED: its hooks/hooks.json command-hooks would run in the Node dispatch process (outside bwrap). INERT for the SDK — both real-SDK factories load plugins:[{path: getPluginPath()}] (the platform-deployed root), never this copy (ADR-093 trust boundary).

REST endpoints for auth, sessions, and agent control. JWT authentication, OAuth providers, session tokens
Executes agent workflows with full orchestration tools
Discovers and loads skills, agents, commands from plugin directory
Guards tool calls (blocks commits to main, rm -rf, etc.) AND injects additive context — phase-surface hints (ADR-070) and declarative skill context_queries (ADR-086).
61 workflow skills — brainstorm, plan, work, review, compound, etc.
65 domain agents across 8 departments
Conventions, learnings, ADRs, specs, plans, brainstorms
Classifies intent and routes to workflow skills
Populates knowledge-base from existing codebase
Lists all available commands, skills, and agents
Explores requirements with domain leader assessment
Creates implementation plans with research and domain review
Executes plans with incremental commits and test-first
Multi-agent code review with 8 parallel reviewers
Captures learnings and promotes to constitution
Validates artifacts, creates PR, manages merge lifecycle
Full autonomous pipeline: plan, work, review, compound, ship
ADR lifecycle and C4 diagram generation
Engineering assessment, architecture decision detection
Marketing assessment, content opportunities
Product strategy, UX flow analysis
Architectural compliance and ADR coverage check
Validation-gates classifier-skill block edits (no-regression + target-passes)
Generates the L1 dep-cruiser import-boundary gate (client→server-secret) plus the two-stage pull_request→workflow_run auto-recovery workflows (ADR-074) into the product codebase CI
Users, BYOK-encrypted API keys, conversation sessions

Severity-ranked operational-notification store (ADR-085, #6007). Mutable read/act/archive state, workspace-grain, Owner-shared reads — SEPARATE from the email-triage WORM statutory ledger. Content-minimized (title + source_ref ids only). GET /api/inbox merges it with email-triage into one severity-ordered feed (statutory pinned first).

Owner-private beta-tester / prospect CRM (ADR-102, #6165). Third-party contact PII + dual-lens conversation notes + append-only stage-transition history. Owner-only RLS; writes RPC-only via auth.uid()-pinned SECURITY DEFINER functions; the cro/cpo agents read AND write via the crm_* MCP tools (agent-native parity). 24-month retention; Art. 17 via ON DELETE CASCADE + crm_erase_contact.
Zero-trust inbound access — no exposed ports

Cluster of Hetzner Cloud hosts (Nomad clients, spread placement) running web app + CLI engine containers; per-user worktrees on host-local NVMe (ADR-068). At first boot a fresh host pulls the web app image from GHCR and extracts its baked bootstrap scripts + hooks.json.tmpl via docker cp (ADR-080, #5921)

Durable trigger layer for server-side agents — cron, oneshot, and HTTP-armed reminders. Extracted to its own single-host private-net node so exactly-one-scheduler is enforced by topology, unblocking active-active web (ADR-030, ADR-100, #6178)

platform.infra.inngestPostgres.technology: PostgreSQL — dedicated EU project, Supavisor session pooler :5432

description.txt: Inngest config + run history. Dedicated project isolates connection budget + fate from the main app DB (#5450). Public tables RLS-locked; reachable only as the postgres owner (ADR-030 I8)

platform.infra.inngestPostgres.title: Supabase PostgreSQL (Inngest)

description.txt: Inngest durable queue + run-state. AOF on the persistent volume survives a host re-provision — the armed-reminder durability fix (#5450)

Co-located stateless sticky router (in each web-host process, not a separate box) — resolves a session's owning host from the per-user worktree lease at the WS-upgrade handshake; local ⇒ serve, remote ⇒ proxy over one-way-TLS private net. Control ops are host-local (no cross-host forwarding). Lease in Postgres (ADR-068 user-sticky amendment)
Placement, health-reschedule, and rolling deploys across the Hetzner cluster (ADR-068 Phase 4a)

platform.infra.gitDataStore.technology: Bare git repos over private net

description.txt: Shared bare git repos (objects/refs) over the private net; per-user worktrees stay host-local on NVMe. The git-data host holds the per-ref monotonic max generation and writer-side-CAS-rejects stale lease writes (ADR-068)

platform.infra.gitDataStore.title: Shared git-data

description.txt: Self-hosted EU Redis — ADR-059 replay buffer + session/control state; network-reachable, per-workspace key namespacing, TLS + requirepass. DISTINCT node from the loopback Inngest Redis (ADR-068 Phase 4a)

1mkp4dq.title: GET /api/crm/* RLS-owner-scoped read routes (board/funnel/detail); crm_get_contact_detail logs each PII read to beta_contact_access_log (Art. 5(2))

13equcc.title: Conversation PII origin — the operator captures notes about this third party

1m7xxtx.title: fix-constraints Stage B (workflow_run, PRIVILEGED, contents:write): opens a data-only draft follow-up PR via Git Data API — never checks out the untrusted tree (ADR-074)

158f4qm.title: Loads plugin from the PLATFORM-DEPLOYED root (getPluginPath(), /app/shared/plugins/soleur) — never the connected-repo workspace copy (ADR-093)

dw5156.title: IGNORED by the loader (trust boundary): the committed workspace plugins/soleur/ is never the SDK load source — getPluginPath() (deployed root) is. Loading it would exec the repo's hooks.json in-process + shadow platform fixes (ADR-093, #4826)

pev1rc.title: Reads context_queries artifacts (skill-scoped injection, web in-process)

1pcz21n.title: Auth and data; per-call audit_github_token_use write for every GitHub App-token use (reads AND Workstream issue writes, Art. 30 PA-16, ADR-109)

1lablqd.title: Daily org cost/usage report (Admin API, cost-report cron)

s5rt2b.title: invoice skill: create/finalize/send/chase via hosted Stripe MCP — OAuth, operator's OWN account; distinct credential plane from the webapp -> stripe billing edge; test-mode only in v1 (ADR-107)

x9g5xx.title: Workstream tab: reads connected-repo issues (REST) + Project v2 board Status (GraphQL); AND writes issues — create / title / label-driven status / close-reopen — via the audited GitHub App-token client (ADR-109). The board is the canonical Status store (ADR-097); a status write is a label mutation that the board-status-sync workflow (github-internal) mirrors. owner/repo/installation resolve from the active workspace (ADR-044), never request input.

zk88vr.title: Statutory triage notifications (single configured recipient; reads are Owner-shared per ADR-066)

98nfq6.title: Ships journald + host_metrics via Vector (arm64, aarch64 build) to the isolated Logs source 2457081; token in soleur-inngest/prd (ADR-100 Phase-1 caveat resolved, #6197)

1x8xht5.title: Ships journald + host_metrics via Vector to the shared Logs source 2457081 (per-host host_name discriminator; token BETTERSTACK_LOGS_TOKEN in soleur/prd); ungated from web_colocate_inngest so every web host ships logs post-ADR-100 (#6396)

1umlahd.title: Hosts (dedicated single-host node, private-net 10.0.1.40; removed from web cloud-init — ADR-100, #6178)

wqy5d1.title: Pulls app image + baked bootstrap scripts/hooks + the cosign .sig at boot/deploy — zot-primary, dark-launch gated (attempts zot only when configured + /v2/-reachable + login ok); the SAME @sha256 digest dual-pushed from CI; verify is offline against the unchanged pinned root, no live sigstore call (ADR-096, #6122)

kwxxt8.title: Atomic fallback pull when zot is unconfigured/unreachable — dual-pushed + break-glass through the Phase-5 soak, then retired; authenticated (read:packages), immutable @sha256 digest, offline cosign verify (#5921, #5887, #6005)

1tk9nxp.title: Origin uptime probe at the LB-fronted app.soleur.ai surface (betteruptime_monitor.app/soleur_apex) — covers web-1 (the app A-record → web-1, dns.tf); the #5933 per-host web-<n> probe was RETIRED (CF 521, no per-host origin vhost). web-2 warm standby has NO standing uptime coverage — its dead-boot page is the #6396 Sentry terminal-stage issue-alert

1fag93g.title: Keyless-signs the released app image digest (cosign, OIDC→Fulcio→Rekor) (#5933 Item 4)

o5ge9n.title: Injects secrets — dedicated host's boot credential is scoped to the SEPARATE Doppler project `soleur-inngest` (its own `prd` root holds only inngest secrets: Postgres URI, freshly-rotated signing/event keys, Redis pw), NOT a `prd` branch config that would inherit all ~116 soleur/prd secrets (ADR-100, #6178; mirrors the soleur-registry isolation #6122)

allv0o.title: Injects the registry-host boot credential (scoped to the isolated soleur-registry project's prd root — the 3 admitted secrets ZOT_PULL/PUSH_TOKEN + BETTERSTACK_LOGS_TOKEN, no soleur/prd inheritance)

1rlw7uv.title: Ships disk-state observability — df% of /var/lib/zot + resize2fs before/after + zot health — as one SOLEUR_ZOT_DISK event, plus a SOLEUR_PRIVATE_NIC private-NIC convergence event (#6415), to the isolated Logs source 2457081; token in soleur-registry/prd, queried via betterstack-query.sh --grep SOLEUR_ZOT_DISK|SOLEUR_PRIVATE_NIC (#6244/#6415)

wszstx.title: Polls the SOLEUR_ZOT_DISK + SOLEUR_PRIVATE_NIC Logs sources (ClickHouse SQL via betterstack-query.sh) for the zot restart-loop recurrence alarm, the private-NIC convergence alarm (#6415), and follow-through soak probes

18nh7m3.title: Mints packages:read installation token every 20 min (ADR-088)

The Logs source receives SOLEUR_ZOT_DISK self-reports from the deny-all registry host and is polled from GitHub Actions via betterstack-query.sh for the zot restart-loop recurrence alarm (#6291) + follow-through soak probes — log-content recurrence alarms are in-repo GH-cron pollers, not native Better Stack alerts (ADR-096).

Severity-ranked operational-notification store (ADR-085, #6007). Mutable read/act/archive state, workspace-grain, Owner-shared reads — SEPARATE from the email-triage WORM statutory ledger. Content-minimized (title + source_ref ids only). GET /api/inbox merges it with email-triage into one severity-ordered feed (statutory pinned first).

Owner-private beta-tester / prospect CRM (ADR-102, #6165). Third-party contact PII + dual-lens conversation notes + append-only stage-transition history. description.txt: Zero-trust inbound access — no exposed ports

Co-located stateless sticky router (in each web-host process, not a separate box) — resolves a session's owning host from the per-user worktree lease at the WS-upgrade handshake; local ⇒ serve, remote ⇒ proxy over one-way-TLS private net. Control ops are host-local (no cross-host forwarding). Lease in Postgres (ADR-068 user-sticky amendment)

Cluster of Hetzner Cloud hosts (Nomad clients, spread placement) running web app + CLI engine containers; per-user worktrees on host-local NVMe (ADR-068). At first boot a fresh host pulls the web app image from GHCR and extracts its baked bootstrap scripts + hooks.json.tmpl via docker cp (ADR-080, #5921)

Durable trigger layer for server-side agents — cron, oneshot, and HTTP-armed reminders. Extracted to its own single-host private-net node so exactly-one-scheduler is enforced by topology, unblocking active-active web (ADR-030, ADR-100, #6178)

PRIVATE GHCR registry (ghcr.io/jikig-ai/soleur-web-platform + soleur-inngest-bootstrap) — the ORIGINAL host pull source, now the DUAL-PUSH + break-glass FALLBACK during the migration to self-hosted zot (ADR-096, #6122); retired from the pull path after the Phase-5 soak (App tokens can't pull GHCR, so it cannot deliver a zero-touch machine credential — ADR-088 superseded). Still bakes the host bootstrap scripts + hooks.json.tmpl, extracted via docker cp at first boot to keep cloud-init user_data under Hetzner's 32,768-byte cap (ADR-080, #5921).

OCI-native container registry (zot) on a dedicated Hetzner host (cx33, 4 vCPU / 8 GB, hel1, volume-backed) on the private network (10.0.1.30:5000), behind a deny-all-public firewall — the zero-touch machine-credential registry that replaces GHCR on the pull path (ADR-096, #6122). Sized cx33 in hel1 (#6288 — the plan's cx32 target does not exist as a Hetzner type; cx33 is the real 8 GB type, offered in hel1 not nbg1) with an ADR-062 --memory=7168m cap so zot's ~35 GB store boot scan cannot host-OOM restart-loop the box. The 60 GB store volume is a disposable GHCR mirror: the nbg1→hel1 migration recreates it fresh and it re-fills from GHCR on the next CI dual-push. Hosts pull the SAME @sha256 digests here (crane-copy from CI preserves them); cosign verifies the zot-stored .sig offline against the unchanged pinned root. Plain-HTTP on the private net (integrity via cosign digest-pinning, not TLS); dark-launch gated with an atomic GHCR fallback until the Phase-5 soak retires GHCR push.
Users, BYOK-encrypted API keys, conversation sessions
